lines.speaker: Nathifa
lines.text: Hello again! It's so nice to be out of Menaphos. Once again, thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't mention it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Nathifa
lines.text: Hello! You must be the adventurer that helped us get out of Menaphos. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't mention it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you have for sale?

lines.speaker: Nathifa
lines.text: Take a look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What were things like in Menaphos?

lines.speaker: Nathifa
lines.text: Not great. I'd just gone over there to purchase some goods from the market. When I got back to the gates, they'd been sealed shut.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the guards wouldn't let you back?

lines.speaker: Nathifa
lines.text: They were still allowing citizens from Sophanem to return at that point, but I foolishly chose to stay. I was scared of catching the pox.

lines.speaker: Nathifa
lines.text: By the time I'd changed my mind, they'd stopped letting anyone through. Thankfully, I had some friends there with a spare bed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess not everyone was as lucky?

lines.speaker: Nathifa
lines.text: No. Many didn't have anywhere to go. Violence broke out in some parts of the city.

lines.speaker: Nathifa
lines.text: They quickly put a stop to it in the Imperial and Merchant quarters, but there was still some unrest in the Workers district when I was finally able to leave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry you had go through all of that. Menaphos definitely took a hard stance on the plagues.

lines.speaker: Nathifa
lines.text: They were brutal. They even shut off the port from the rest of the city over fears of the plague getting in that way. Many of their own citizens are still trapped there.

lines.speaker: Nathifa
lines.text: Anyway, let's not talk any more of this. Is there anything else I can do for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Nathifa
lines.text: Until next time.
